title: Israel destroys five out of six bridges linking south Lebanon to rest of country
description: As it engages in heated battles with pro-Iran militant group Hezbollah in a number of Lebanese border villages, Israel has bombed at least five of the six principal bridges across a river dividing the country's south. It adds to widespread fears that Israel intends to permanently depopulate the region as a million people have been displaced by the fighting. FRANCE 24's Claire Paccalin tells us more from Beirut.

title: 'Growing mistrust and disagreement' over Trump declaring war with Iran
description: In a surprise U-turn, US President Donald Trump has extended a deadline he had given to Iran over the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, adding five days to the initial 48-hour window. He had threatened to strike Iranian power plants were the Strait not to be reopened. For FRANCE 24's Philip Turle, it could be partly explained by the 'growing mistrust and disagreement' over his decision to start a war with Iran.

title: Jewish charity ambulances set ablaze in London in suspected hate crime
description: Four ambulances belonging to a Jewish charity were set on fire early on March 23 in London. British police are investigating the arson attack as a hate crime. This comes amid a rise in antisemitic attacks in the UK since the October 7 Hamas attacks, and are suspected to amplify due to the war in the Middle East. FRANCE 24's Benedicte Paviot tells us more from London.

title: 'I'm proud when we can turn our diversity into strength': Author Jens Christian Grøndahl
description: One of the world's top authors, whose books have been translated into more than 25 languages, has spoken to FRANCE 24 about his pride in being European. The Danish author Jens Christian Grøndahl, who has had notable success at home and in France, says he loves the fact that in Europe you can travel for just a few hours and be in a completely different cultural setting, but at the same time the people share the same roots.

title: Trump extends his deadline for Iran to reopen Strait of Hormuz
description: US President Donald Trump has extended his deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. The U.S. will hold off on striking power plant strikes for five days, close near the end of the original forty-eight hour deadline. He also said the US and Iran were having 'very good' conversations - something that Tehran has denied. FRANCE 24's Philip Turle breaks down these new developments.

title: New spat of settler violence in West Bank, leaving 10 Palestinians injured
description: At least 10 Palestinians were injured in attacks on March 23 in the occupied West Bank by Israeli settlers. The group rampaged through nearby villages after holding a funeral for a 18 year-old settler killed in a car crash the day before. The violence came as Israel’s government presses ahead with new settlements in the occupied West Bank .

title: Israel to 'advance targeted ground operations' in Lebanon
description: Israel continues to bomb Lebanon as it tries to root out Hezbollah, which the president has called a collective punishment. After having destroyed several bridges linking southern Lebanon to the rest of the country, Israeli forces are 'advancing targeted ground operations' as they claim the operation against Hezbollah 'has only just begun'. Meanwhile, the death toll in Lebanon has risen to over 1,000 killed and at least 2,700 wounded.

title: European far-right leaders gather in Budapest ahead of Hungarian elections
description: Far-right leaders of Europe are gathering in Budapest for the so-called European Parliament group Patriots' grand assembly. The gathering is not only a show of force for Europe's populists: it's also a show of support for Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban, ahead of national elections on April 12. FRANCE 24's Gulliver Cragg tells us more from Budapest.

title: Trump backtracks on Iran's deadline to reopen Strait of Hormuz
description: Donald Trump says the US and Iran had 'very good and productive talks' over the weekend. He also said the US would hold off on strikes on Iranian powerplants for five days, just as his initial 48-hour deadline was coming to an end. But the Iranian news agency says there has been no communication between Washington and Tehran.

title: Taiwan opposition leader says that improving ties with China is not anti-US
description: Cheng Li-wun, leader of the Kuomintang, said ‘improving ties with Beijing does not require being anti-US.’

title: Has Iran brought down an ‘unkillable’ US F-35 jet?
description: The supersonic fighter jet is designed to avoid detection by radar and other technologies.

title: Trump postpones military strikes on Iranian power plants for five days
description: Trump postpones strikes on Iranian citing productive talks aimed at easing Middle East hostilities.

title: Iraq pulled into Iran war as US targets Iran-aligned groups
description: Iraq is increasingly caught between the US and Iran as the regional war escalates, deepening economic woes.

title: Threats over Iran war bare Trump efforts to transform free speech: Experts
description: Threat to punish broadcasters for coverage not in public interest comes amid wider roll-backs, analysts say.

title: NYT рассказала, как Нетаньяху смог убедить Трампа начать агрессию против Ирана
description: Глава Моссада Давид Барнеа убедил руководство Израиля и США в способности израильской разведки спровоцировать протесты и хаос в Иране и в оперативном порядке свергнуть власти Исламской Республики. Об этом информирует газета The New York Times (NYT), ссылаясь на источники в американском и израильском истеблишменте.

title: Israel has sufficient oil and petroleum reserves, for now — energy expert
description:
But with costs rising, gasoline prices could go up by NIS 1 per liter, or $1.21 per gallon, next month unless Finance Ministry cuts fuel tax to soften the pinch
The post Israel has sufficient oil and petroleum reserves, for now  energy expert appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: IEA chief: Energy crisis due to Iran war a ‘major, major threat’ to global economy
description:
Closure of the Strait of Hormuz and attacks on energy infrastructure more disruptive than 1970s oil shocks, Birol says; Trump and Iran threaten to start targeting power plants
The post IEA chief: Energy crisis due to Iran war a major, major threat to global economy appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: Iran warns it will hit Israeli, Gulf power plants if US targets Iranian electric stations
description:
'If you hit electricity, we hit electricity,' IRGC says, warns will also mine Gulf waters if invaded; Israel launches extensive strikes on Tehran as Iran continues missile attacks
The post Iran warns it will hit Israeli, Gulf power plants if US targets Iranian electric stations appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: Netanyahu said frustrated that Mossad promise it could instigate Iran uprising has fallen short
description:
NYT says in leadup to war, spy agency touted plans to instigate mass resistance that could trigger regime's collapse; instead, rulers have dug in and revolution appears unlikely
The post Netanyahu said frustrated that Mossad promise it could instigate Iran uprising has fallen short appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: Four London Jewish community ambulances set ablaze outside synagogue in hate crime
description:
Police looking for three suspects seen in footage pouring gasoline on Hatzola emergency vehicles parked at Golders Green's Machzike Hadath Synagogue, setting them on fire; no injuries reported
The post Four London Jewish community ambulances set ablaze outside synagogue in hate crime appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: Slovenian PM beats pro-Israel rival, despite alleged election interference by Israeli firm
description:
Robert Golob, who accuses Jewish state of 'genocide,' clinches win over Trump admirer Janez Jansa; Israel's Black Cube claimed to be behind video suggesting government corruption
The post Slovenian PM beats pro-Israel rival, despite alleged election interference by Israeli firm appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: US carrier Ford to go to port temporarily after fire
description: The USS Gerald R. Ford, deployed in operations against Iran, is expected to temporarily go to Souda Bay on the Greek island of Crete, officials said.

title: Number of US troops wounded in war against Iran rises to about 200
description: The U.S. military’s Central Command said the vast majority of those wounded had suffered minor injuries and 180 troops had already returned to duty.

title: Ukraine opens battlefield AI data to allies in world-first move
description: Ukraine is giving international partners and defense companies access to its trove of real combat data to train AI models for autonomous drone systems.
